comment_text.sn24.4:1.3: This is a saying of the annihilationists (ucchedavāda) at SN 22.55:1.3.

root_text.sn24.4:1.2: “Kismiṁ nu kho, bhikkhave, sati, kiṁ upādāya, kiṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.4:1.3: ‘no cassaṁ, no ca me siyā, nābhavissa, na me bhavissatī’”ti?
root_text.sn24.4:2.1: “Rūpe kho, bhikkhave, sati, rūpaṁ upādāya, rūpaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.4:2.2: ‘no cassaṁ, no ca me siyā, nābhavissa, na me bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.sn24.4:2.6: viññāṇe sati, viññāṇaṁ upādāya, viññāṇaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.4:2.7: ‘no cassaṁ, no ca me siyā, nābhavissa, na me bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.sn24.4:3.4: api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.4:3.5: ‘no cassaṁ, no ca me siyā, nābhavissa, na me bhavissatī’”ti?
root_text.sn24.4:3.12: api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.4:3.13: ‘no cassaṁ, no ca me siyā, nābhavissa, na me bhavissatī’”ti?
root_text.sn24.4:3.15: “Yampidaṁ diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā tampi niccaṁ vā aniccaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn24.4:3.17: api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.4:3.18: ‘no cassaṁ, no ca me siyā, nābhavissa, na me bhavissatī’”ti?
root_text.sn24.4:4.1: “Yato kho, bhikkhave, ariyasāvakassa imesu ca ṭhānesu kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti, dukkhepissa kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti …pe… dukkhanirodhagāminiyā paṭipadāyapissa kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti—
root_text.sn24.4:4.2: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako sotāpanno avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyano”ti.

translation_text.sn24.4:1.2: “Mendicants, when what exists, because of grasping what and insisting on what, does the view arise:
translation_text.sn24.4:1.3: ‘I might not be, and it might not be mine. I will not be, and it will not be mine’?”
translation_text.sn24.4:1.4: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn24.4:2.1: “When form exists, because of grasping form and insisting on form, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.4:2.2: ‘I might not be, and it might not be mine. I will not be, and it will not be mine.’
translation_text.sn24.4:2.6: consciousness exists, because of grasping consciousness and insisting on consciousness, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.4:2.7: ‘I might not be, and it might not be mine. I will not be, and it will not be mine.’
translation_text.sn24.4:3.15: “That which is seen, heard, thought, known, attained, sought, and explored by the mind: is that permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn24.4:3.17: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would such a view arise?”
translation_text.sn24.4:4.1: “When a noble disciple has given up doubt regarding these six grounds, and has given up doubt in suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation,
translation_text.sn24.4:4.2: they’re called a noble disciple who is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.”
